summary: A mobile-friendly directory app called 'Mesa County Hidden Eats' for discovering local restaurants and eateries with gamification features, random discovery, and enhanced vibe filtering to drive user engagement and monetization
architectural_notes: Directory-style application for browsing local eateries
Warm, rustic 'Western Colorado' aesthetic with earthy tones and clean typography
Replace dropdown menus with simple buttons to avoid component errors
Include placeholder restaurant data for Grand Junction and Fruita for valid initial state
User profile storage needed for visited status, ratings, and private notes per restaurant
Geo-location services required for 'Near Me' feature using browser GPS API
Vibe attributes (pet-friendly, riverfront trail, date-friendly) need to be added to restaurant data model
Business claim feature should capture owner contact information for future monetization
Seasonal availability field needed in restaurant data model with date range support
known_issues: Crash on draft page due to Select.Item error in dropdown menus
All restaurants currently display the same placeholder image instead of unique images
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- A photo of a modern colorful art studio with a small bar area serving drinks (hybrid-art-studio.dim_800x600.png)

existing_features.title: Create a mobile-responsive restaurant directory interface that displays a list of local restaurants and eateries with their basic information (name, cuisine type, brief description)
Add search functionality that allows users to filter restaurants by name or cuisine type

Design a visually cohesive interface with a warm, inviting aesthetic that emphasizes local food discovery through earthy tones, appetizing imagery treatment, and clear typography suitable for a culinary directory
Display generated restaurant placeholder images in the directory using static assets from frontend/public/assets/generated

Update the color palette in frontend/src/index.css to reflect a warm, rustic 'Western Colorado' aesthetic using earthy tones such as desert sand, canyon red, sage brush green, and weathered wood brown while maintaining the OKLCH color format
Update typography in frontend/tailwind.config.js to use clean, rustic-appropriate font selections that evoke a Western Colorado aesthetic while maintaining excellent readability
Enhance frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx header and footer styling to reinforce the Western Colorado rustic aesthetic through subtle design touches such as border treatments or background textures
Update frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx hero section to feature Western Colorado-inspired visual elements and messaging that emphasize the local rustic character

Refine frontend/src/components/RestaurantCard.tsx styling to incorporate rustic design elements such as warm borders, earthy backgrounds, and typography that aligns with the Western Colorado aesthetic
Verify component rendering order in frontend/src/App.tsx and frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx to ensure queries using useActor only execute after authentication providers are mounted and ready
Add error boundaries and loading states in frontend/src/components/RestaurantList.tsx and frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx to gracefully handle authentication and query failures
Rewrite frontend/src/components/SearchBar.tsx to remove the dropdown Select component for cuisine filtering and replace it with simple button elements for each cuisine type
Update frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts so useGetAllRestaurants returns the actual restaurant list from the backend instead of an empty array
Verify frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx removes any remaining dropdown dependencies and renders correctly with the button-based search interface
Update frontend/src/components/RestaurantCard.tsx to display the 'Hidden Gem' tag as a visual badge when present on a restaurant
Update frontend/src/components/RestaurantCard.tsx to display the full street address for each restaurant below the location city information

Add a 'Visited' button to frontend/src/components/RestaurantCard.tsx that changes visual appearance (color) when the restaurant is marked as visited, calls the backend addVisitedRestaurant method on click using the authenticated user's identity, and queries the backend isRestaurantVisited method to initialize the button state

Create React Query mutation hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts for marking restaurants as visited (useMarkVisited) and querying visited status (useIsVisited) that call the corresponding backend methods with the authenticated user's Principal

Create a new frontend/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage.tsx route component that displays full restaurant information, a 5-star interactive rating component allowing users to select 1-5 stars, and a text input field for private notes with save functionality that calls the backend rating and note methods
Update frontend/src/components/RestaurantCard.tsx to link to the new restaurant detail page route when clicked, passing the restaurant name or ID as a route parameter

Add React Query hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts for saving and fetching user ratings (useSaveRating, useGetRating) and private notes (useSaveNote, useGetNote) that call the corresponding backend methods with the authenticated user's Principal and restaurant identifier
Register the restaurant detail page route in frontend/src/App.tsx using TanStack Router with a dynamic route parameter for restaurant identification

Add a 'Find Gems Near Me' button to frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx that requests the user's geolocation and filters restaurants by proximity, showing only restaurants within a configurable radius (default 10 miles)

Add vibe filter buttons to frontend/src/components/SearchBar.tsx for 'Pet-Friendly Patio', 'Near the Riverfront Trail', and 'Great for a Date' that can be toggled independently alongside existing cuisine filters

Update frontend/src/hooks/useRestaurantSearch.ts to include vibe filter logic that filters restaurants based on isPetFriendly, isNearRiverfrontTrail, and isGreatForDate attributes when vibe filters are active

Add a 'Claim My Business' link at the bottom of frontend/src/components/RestaurantCard.tsx and frontend/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage.tsx that opens a contact form or email link allowing restaurant owners to express interest in featured placement

Add a 'Palisade Fruit Season' toggle button to frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx that, when enabled, highlights and prioritizes seasonal establishments (orchards and stands open June–October) and dims or filters out non-seasonal venues

Add seasonal badges to frontend/src/components/RestaurantCard.tsx that display 'Open June-October' or custom month ranges for restaurants marked as seasonal, with prominent visual styling during the active season months

Update the 'Visited' button in frontend/src/components/RestaurantCard.tsx to turn solid green and display 'Visited' text when clicked, calling the useMarkVisited mutation hook to persist the visited status with the authenticated user's Principal and restaurant identifier

Add an 'I'm Feeling Lucky' button at the top of frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx that randomly selects one restaurant marked with isHiddenGem: true and navigates the user to that restaurant's detail page route (/restaurant/$name)

Create a new frontend/src/pages/ProfilePage.tsx route component that displays a 'My Progress' dashboard showing gamification statistics, including the percentage of Mesa County restaurants the authenticated user has visited (e.g., 'You've explored 15% of Mesa County!'), total restaurants visited count, and total restaurants available count
Register the Profile page route in frontend/src/App.tsx using TanStack Router at path '/profile' so users can navigate to their progress dashboard
Add a navigation link or button in frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx header that links to the Profile page (/profile) for authenticated users to access their progress dashboard

Add vibe filter buttons for 'Live Music', 'Dog-Friendly Patio', and 'Great for Groups' to frontend/src/components/SearchBar.tsx that can be toggled independently alongside the existing vibe filters (Pet-Friendly Patio, Near the Riverfront Trail, Great for a Date)

Update frontend/src/hooks/useRestaurantSearch.ts to include filter logic for the new vibe attributes isLiveMusic, isDogFriendly, and isGreatForGroups, filtering restaurants based on these attributes when the corresponding vibe filters are active

Update frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx to include state management for the three new vibe filters (Live Music, Dog-Friendly Patio, Great for Groups) and pass these states to the SearchBar and restaurant search hook

Create a new frontend/src/pages/PassportPage.tsx route component that displays a 'Mesa County Passport' page with a prominent progress bar showing the percentage of restaurants the authenticated user has visited (e.g., 'You've visited 12 of 80 restaurants - 15% complete'), total restaurants visited count, and total restaurants available count
Register the Mesa County Passport page route in frontend/src/App.tsx using TanStack Router at path '/passport' so users can navigate to their passport progress page

Add a navigation link or button in frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx header that links to the Passport page (/passport) labeled 'My Passport' for authenticated users to access their passport progress

Add a 'Surprise Me' button to frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx (home screen) that requests the user's geolocation, filters Hidden Gem restaurants by proximity within a configurable radius (default 10 miles), randomly selects one from the filtered set, and navigates the user to that restaurant's detail page route (/restaurant/$name)
Verify frontend/src/components/RestaurantCard.tsx correctly renders the imageURL from each restaurant object as the image source, ensuring each restaurant displays its unique image
Update frontend/src/components/SearchBar.tsx to include 'Hybrid' as a cuisine filter button alongside existing cuisine options, maintaining the same visual style and toggle behavior
Update frontend/src/hooks/useRestaurantSearch.ts to include 'Hybrid' in the cuisine filtering logic so restaurants with cuisine type 'Hybrid' are returned when the Hybrid filter is active

new_feature_requests.summary: Create mobile-friendly directory app structure
Implement restaurant/eatery listing functionality
Add search and filtering capabilities for directory
Implement backend data model and storage for restaurant entries including fields for name, cuisine type, description, location, and contact information

Verify and fix Internet Identity authentication flow in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts to ensure proper initialization, delegation validation, and state synchronization without race conditions
Ensure React Query client in frontend/src/main.tsx is properly configured with appropriate staleTime and cacheTime settings to prevent conflicts during component hydration
Fix actor initialization timing in frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts to ensure the backend actor is created only after Internet Identity authentication completes successfully
Rewrite app code from scratch removing all dropdown menus and replacing with simple buttons to prevent Select.Item errors
Add placeholder restaurant data for Grand Junction and Fruita to ensure valid app state on load
Add placeholder restaurant data in backend/main.mo with at least 5-8 sample restaurants from Grand Junction and Fruita including names, cuisine types, descriptions, locations, and contact information

Replace the existing restaurant data in backend/main.mo with exactly 5 Mesa County restaurants: Pêche (Palisade) with fine dining and locally sourced description, Hot Tomato Pizza (Fruita) with legendary pizza and bike-friendly description, Taco Party (Grand Junction) with creative local tacos description, Best Slope Coffee (Fruita) with artisan coffee and community hub description, and Bin 707 Foodbar (Grand Junction) with high-end seasonal Colorado cuisine description
Add a complete street address field to each of the 5 restaurants in backend/main.mo using realistic Mesa County addresses appropriate for each city (Palisade, Fruita, Grand Junction)
Add a 'Hidden Gem' tag field to the Restaurant type in backend/main.mo and set it to true for all 5 restaurants

Replace the existing restaurant data in backend/main.mo with exactly 5 Mesa County restaurants with complete details: (1) Pêche in Palisade with cuisine type 'Fine Dining', description 'Fine dining, locally sourced', a realistic Palisade street address, and isHiddenGem set to true; (2) Hot Tomato Pizza in Fruita with cuisine type 'Pizza', description 'Legendary pizza, bike-friendly', a realistic Fruita street address, and isHiddenGem set to true; (3) Taco Party in Grand Junction with cuisine type 'Mexican', description 'Creative local tacos', a realistic Grand Junction street address, and isHiddenGem set to true; (4) Best Slope Coffee in Fruita with cuisine type 'Cafe', description 'Artisan coffee and community hub', a realistic Fruita street address, and isHiddenGem set to true; (5) Bin 707 Foodbar in Grand Junction with cuisine type 'Fine Dining', description 'High-end seasonal Colorado cuisine', a realistic Grand Junction street address, and isHiddenGem set to true

Add a Principal-indexed Map type to backend/main.mo to store user-specific data including visited restaurant status, with methods to mark a restaurant as visited for a user (addVisitedRestaurant) and to check if a restaurant is visited by a user (isRestaurantVisited)

Add Principal-indexed Map types to backend/main.mo to store user ratings (1-5 stars) and private notes for each restaurant, with methods to save a rating (saveRating), get a rating (getRating), save a note (saveNote), and get a note (getNote) for the authenticated user and specified restaurant
Add 'Near Me' geo-fencing feature with a button to find restaurants near the user's current location using GPS coordinates
Add 'Vibe' filters including Pet-Friendly Patio, Near the Riverfront Trail, and Great for a Date as filterable attributes for restaurants
Add 'Claim My Business' link at the bottom of restaurant listings for restaurant owners to express interest in featured placement
Add 'Palisade Fruit Season' seasonal toggle mode to highlight orchards and stands that are only open June–October

Add a latitude and longitude field to the Restaurant type in backend/main.mo for all existing restaurants using realistic Mesa County coordinates (Palisade ~39.1097°N 108.3526°W, Fruita ~39.1586°N 108.7287°W, Grand Junction ~39.0639°N 108.5506°W)

Add vibe attribute fields to the Restaurant type in backend/main.mo including isPetFriendly (Bool), isNearRiverfrontTrail (Bool), and isGreatForDate (Bool), and populate these fields for all existing restaurants based on their characteristics

Add a seasonal attribute field to the Restaurant type in backend/main.mo called isPalisadeFruitSeason (Bool) to indicate if the establishment is an orchard or stand that is only open June–October, and add a seasonalMonths field (array of month numbers 1-12) to specify exact months of operation

Verify frontend/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage.tsx displays full restaurant information, includes a 5-star interactive rating selector that allows users to select 1-5 stars, and provides a text input field labeled 'My Private Notes' where users can type and save personal notes about what they ate

Add three new restaurant entries to the backend/main.mo restaurant data: (1) Taco Party in Grand Junction with cuisine type 'Mexican', a realistic Grand Junction street address, appropriate coordinates (~39.0639°N 108.5506°W), isHiddenGem set to true, and relevant vibe attributes; (2) Best Slope Coffee in Fruita with cuisine type 'Cafe', a realistic Fruita street address, appropriate coordinates (~39.1586°N 108.7287°W), isHiddenGem set to true, and relevant vibe attributes; (3) Bin 707 Foodbar in Grand Junction with cuisine type 'Fine Dining', a realistic Grand Junction street address, appropriate coordinates (~39.0639°N 108.5506°W), isHiddenGem set to true, and relevant vibe attributes
Add an 'I'm Feeling Lucky' button at the top of the directory page that randomly selects one Hidden Gem restaurant and navigates the user to its detail page
Create a 'My Progress' dashboard/profile tab that displays gamification stats including percentage of Mesa County restaurants explored based on visited restaurants
Add additional vibe filters for 'Live Music', 'Dog-Friendly Patio', and 'Great for Groups' to the existing vibe filter system

Add vibe attribute fields isLiveMusic (Bool), isDogFriendly (Bool), and isGreatForGroups (Bool) to the Restaurant type in backend/main.mo, and populate these fields for all existing restaurants based on their characteristics
Create a 'Mesa County Passport' tab/page that displays a progress bar showing the user's visited restaurant count as a percentage of total Mesa County restaurants
Add a 'Surprise Me' button on the home screen that randomly selects one Hidden Gem restaurant based on the user's current geolocation and navigates to its detail page

Update restaurant data in backend/main.mo so each restaurant has a unique imageURL: Hot Tomato Pizza should have a vibrant pizza shop image, Best Slope Coffee should have a modern artisan cafe image, and Pêche should have a fine-dining plating image

Update the Restaurant type imageURL field in backend/main.mo for Hot Tomato Pizza to reference a unique vibrant pizza shop image file path (frontend/public/assets/generated/hot-tomato-pizza.dim_800x600.png)

Update the Restaurant type imageURL field in backend/main.mo for Best Slope Coffee to reference a unique modern artisan cafe image file path (frontend/public/assets/generated/best-slope-coffee.dim_800x600.png)
Update the Restaurant type imageURL field in backend/main.mo for Pêche to reference a unique fine-dining plating image file path (frontend/public/assets/generated/peche-plating.dim_800x600.png)

Update the Restaurant type imageURL fields in backend/main.mo for all remaining restaurants (Taco Party, Bin 707 Foodbar) to reference unique image file paths that are distinct from Hot Tomato Pizza, Best Slope Coffee, and Pêche
Add a 'Hybrid' cuisine type category to backend/main.mo for businesses that serve food/drinks but aren't traditional restaurants

Add a new restaurant entry in backend/main.mo: ADDRESS 2650 North Ave. unit 117 Grand Junction, CO 81501, cuisine type 'Hybrid', description including dirty sodas/twisted teas/snacks, appropriate Grand Junction coordinates, isHiddenGem true, vibe attributes for art/crafts/date night/family-friendly, and imageURL referencing a modern colorful art studio with small bar area
Add 'Hybrid' as a new cuisine type option in the Restaurant type definition in backend/main.mo to categorize businesses that serve food/drinks but aren't traditional restaurants

Add a new restaurant entry in backend/main.mo with the following details: name appropriate for the address, cuisine type 'Hybrid', address '2650 North Ave. unit 117 Grand Junction, CO 81501', coordinates approximately 39.0639°N 108.5506°W, description 'Serves dirty sodas, twisted teas, and snacks', isHiddenGem set to true, vibe attributes including art/crafts-focused, date night friendly, and family-friendly, and imageURL pointing to 'frontend/public/assets/generated/hybrid-art-studio.dim_800x600.png'

Render the generated hybrid art studio image (frontend/public/assets/generated/hybrid-art-studio.dim_800x600.png) as a static asset in frontend/src/components/RestaurantCard.tsx using the imageURL field from the new Hybrid restaurant entry
